---
title: "Methodology"
subtitle: "How figures on this site are computed, and the choices behind them."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

This page collects the methodological choices that sit behind the figures on the platform. If any of them surprise you, open an [issue](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/issues) — we would rather hear about a disagreement than watch a reader walk away confused.

## Guiding commitments

1. **Public-domain data only.** [USDA-AMS Market News](https://www.ams.usda.gov/market-news/livestock-poultry-grain) via the [MARS API](https://mymarketnews.ams.usda.gov/mars-api), BLS CPI-U via the [public v2 API](https://www.bls.gov/developers/api_signature_v2.htm), and [USDA NASS QuickStats](https://quickstats.nass.usda.gov/). All three are U.S. public domain under [17 U.S.C. § 105](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/17/105). CME futures are link-out only ([feeder-cattle contract specs](https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/agriculture/livestock/feeder-cattle.contractSpecs.html)); third-party compilations of the same underlying public-domain reports are not redistributed (each carries its own licensing terms outside this repo's public-domain commitment). See [`LICENSE-DATA.md`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/LICENSE-DATA.md).
2. **Every figure carries a vintage stamp.** The CPI vintage used to deflate, the date the underlying AMS report was published, and the date the page was last rebuilt — all visible on the page or one click away.
3. **Reproducibility before breadth.** We would rather serve one figure that can be re-computed from source in a few minutes than five figures whose provenance has drifted.
4. **Last-known-good on failure.** A failed pipeline run never overwrites a good snapshot. The site keeps serving the last successful vintage until the failure is investigated. See [GOVERNANCE](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md).

## Live pipelines and their current freshness

Every row below is read at render time from the pipeline's own manifest file, so the "latest vintage" shown is whatever this page picked up during the last site build — not a hand-typed number.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: freshness-table

from pathlib import Path
import json
from IPython.display import Markdown

PROCESSED = Path("..") / "data" / "processed"
MANIFESTS = [
    ("BLS CPI-U (monthly refresh)", PROCESSED / "MANIFEST.json"),
    ("USDA-AMS Clovis auction (weekly refresh)", PROCESSED / "clovis_MANIFEST.json"),
    ("Clovis Era B historical (one-time)", PROCESSED / "clovis_historical_MANIFEST.json"),
    ("CME GF feeder settles (one-time bootstrap)", PROCESSED / "cme_feeders_MANIFEST.json"),
    ("Clovis basis (derived, refreshed on ingest)", PROCESSED / "clovis_basis_MANIFEST.json"),
]

def _row(label, path):
    if not path.exists():
        return f"| {label} | _(no manifest yet)_ | — | — | — |"
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        m = json.load(f)
    entries = m.get("entries", [])
    if not entries:
        return f"| {label} | _(empty manifest)_ | — | — | — |"
    e = entries[-1]
    rows = e.get("rows", "—")
    rows_str = f"{rows:,}" if isinstance(rows, int) else "—"
    sha = (e.get("sha256") or "")[:12]
    return (
        f"| {label} | `{e.get('vintage','—')}` | {rows_str} | "
        f"`{sha}…` | {e.get('written_at_utc','—')} |"
    )

header = (
    "| Pipeline | Latest vintage | Rows committed | Snapshot SHA | Written (UTC) |\n"
    "|---|---|---|---|---|"
)
body = "\n".join(_row(label, path) for label, path in MANIFESTS)
Markdown(header + "\n" + body)
```

## Data sources

| Source | Series or dataset | Pipeline | Refresh cadence | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [BLS](https://www.bls.gov/cpi/) | CPI-U, All Items, U.S. City Average, NSA ([`CUUR0000SA0`](https://data.bls.gov/timeseries/CUUR0000SA0)) | [`pipelines/bls/`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/tree/main/pipelines/bls) | Monthly, 16th 14:00 UTC | **Live** |
| [USDA-AMS Market News](https://www.ams.usda.gov/market-news/livestock-poultry-grain) | Clovis Livestock Auction feeder-cattle report (`AMS_1781`) via [MARS API](https://mymarketnews.ams.usda.gov/mars-api) | [`pipelines/clovis/`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/tree/main/pipelines/clovis) | Weekly, Thursday 18:00 UTC | **Live** |
| USDA-AMS | Additional Market News reports (other auctions, other commodities) | Not yet built | — | *Phase 2 candidate* |
| [USDA NASS](https://quickstats.nass.usda.gov/) | QuickStats — state/county crop and livestock statistics | Not yet built | — | *Phase 2 candidate* |
| [CME GF](https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/agriculture/livestock/feeder-cattle.contractSpecs.html) | Feeder-cattle daily futures settles (8 contract months + Nearby) | [`pipelines/cme_feeders/`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/tree/main/pipelines/cme_feeders); basis derivation [`pipelines/clovis/basis.py`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/pipelines/clovis/basis.py) | One-time bootstrap from a third-party settle workbook; Phase-2 follow-on refreshes from CME directly | **Live (derived basis only)** |

The Clovis weekly series extends back to October 2017 — the floor of USDA-AMS Market News's per-slug archive for this report. The October 2017 → April 2019 portion was a one-time historical reconstruction (read-only after ingest); from April 2019 forward the live MARS pipeline keeps the series current.

## Specific methodology pages

- [CPI deflation and the release-basis vintage](cpi.qmd)
- [Clovis auction pipeline: slug `AMS_1781`, filters, and schema](clovis.qmd)
- [Basis derivation: Clovis cash − CME GF settle](basis.qmd)

## What a methodology page contains

Every methodology page on this site is expected to answer, in order:

1. **What is being measured?** (The underlying concept in plain language.)
2. **Where does the data come from?** (The specific series, the API endpoint, the vintage.)
3. **What transformations are applied?** (Unit conversions, category mappings, deflation, weighting.)
4. **What is deliberately left out?** (Excluded years, excluded categories, known quality caveats.)
5. **Where is the code?** (Link to the pipeline module and the `.qmd` file's source.)
6. **Who to contact?** (A GitHub issue template or a maintainer's handle.)

If a page you are reading does not answer one of these, that is a bug — open an issue.
